title: Brighton man found ‘badly decomposed’ after assisted living didn’t check on him for days, family says
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “Felipe Dip’s body had been decomposing in his room, undiscovered, for approximately nine days,” the family alleged in a new lawsuit.

By Abby Patkin June 17, 2026 | 3:23 PM 1 minute to read To Felipe Dip’s family, the red flags were obvious: The 64-year-old had missed at least 27 meals straight at his assisted living facility in Brighton, and it had been more than a week since a motion-sensing camera in his bedroom detected any movement.
And yet, according to Dip’s family, no one working at Chestnut Park at Cleveland Circle “conducted a welfare check, entered Felipe Dip’s room, or otherwise investigated his whereabouts or well-being.

title: Fan who traveled to Boston from Scotland for World Cup dies suddenly before getting to see match
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Local News “Since Scotland qualified in November, he’s never shut up about it.
He was telling everybody this was his lifelong dream.
” Scotland supporters embrace and celebrate after Scotland defeated Haiti, 1-0, in their Group C opener Saturday night in Foxborough.

title: How do you build a barbecue empire in Boston?
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Food News Andy Husbands spent the last decade turning The Smoke Shop from a Kendall Square startup into a six-location business.
The Smoke Shop is celebrating its 10th anniversary, marking a decade of growth from a single Kendall Square restaurant to six locations across Massachusetts.
Ken Goodman Photography ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: Boston hotels are adopting ships for Sail Boston 2026
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Local News Hotels across the city are adjusting their programming and menus to honor the countries where the ships hail from.
Adirondack III from Boston in front of Boston Harbor Hotel during a previous tall ship celebration.
Boston Harbor Hotel City hotels are busy designing decor, food, and cocktails that compliment the countries of the tall ships visiting Boston next month for Sail Boston 2026.

title: Thanks to Erling Haaland, Boston Stadium witnessed greatness at the World Cup
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: World Cup In clinical fashion, Erling Haaland led Norway to a 4-1 win over Iraq, and in doing so, gave local fans the chance to witness one of the best goalscorers on the planet.
Norway's Erling Haaland celebrates the first of his two goals in a 4-1 win over Iraq in the World Cup on Tuesday in Foxborough.
Barry Chin/Globe Staff By Kevin Slane June 17, 2026 | 1:50 PM 3 minutes to read ShareSend this article to your social connections.

title: The MBTA is loving the World Cup energy, but the escalator rowing? Not so much.
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: World Cup “Adding this to the list of things I’ve never seen before and probably never will again.
” Norway fans on the train heading to "Boston Stadium" (Gillette Stadium) for the World Cup game against Iraq.

Jessica Rinaldi / The Boston Globe By Abby Patkin June 17, 2026 | 1:20 PM 1 minute to read Related: Not to be outdone by Scotland’s Tartan Army, Norway fans in town for the World Cup have charmed Bostonians with their synchronized “rowing” on escalators around the city.
